contents.value: **namespace** — Q# keyword.
**open** — Q# keyword.
**operation** — Q# keyword.
**function** — Q# keyword.
**newtype** — Q# keyword.
**H** — Built-in function/command in Q#.
**X** — Built-in function/command in Q#.
**Y** — Built-in function/command in Q#.
**Z** — Built-in function/command in Q#.
**CNOT** — Built-in function/command in Q#.
**let** binds a value to a name (immutable by default).
**match** performs pattern matching against a value.
**type** defines a new type alias or algebraic data type.
A **record** groups named fields into a product type.
A **variant** (sum type) represents one of several possible shapes.
**Recursion** occurs when a function calls itself to solve smaller subproblems.
**Pattern matching** destructures values and dispatches based on shape.
A **tuple** is a fixed-size product of heterogeneous values.
**Immutable** values cannot be changed after creation.
A **higher-order function** takes functions as arguments or returns them.
**Currying** transforms a multi-argument function into a sequence of single-argument functions.
A **monad** is an abstraction for sequencing computations with context (e.g., Option, Result).
**fold** (reduce) combines elements of a collection into a single result.
**map** applies a function to each element of a collection, producing a new collection.

```qsharp
Qubit
```
The fundamental quantum data type in Q# representing a single quantum bit that exists in a superposition of states. Qubits are allocated using the use statement and cannot be directly copied or inspected.

```qsharp
Result = Zero | One
```
A classical discriminated union type in Q# representing the outcome of a quantum measurement, with two possible values Zero and One corresponding to the computational basis states.

```qsharp
operation M(qubit : Qubit) : Result
```
Performs a single-qubit measurement in the computational (Z) basis, collapsing the qubit superposition to either Zero or One and returning the classical Result value.

```qsharp
operation Rx(theta : Double, qubit : Qubit) : Unit is Adj + Ctl
```
Applies a rotation around the X-axis of the Bloch sphere by the specified angle theta in radians. Used for parameterized quantum circuits and variational quantum algorithms.

```qsharp
operation Rz(theta : Double, qubit : Qubit) : Unit is Adj + Ctl
```
Applies a rotation around the Z-axis of the Bloch sphere by the specified angle theta in radians. Commonly used for phase manipulation in quantum Fourier transforms.

```qsharp
use qubit = Qubit() { ... }
```
Allocates one or more fresh qubits initialized in the |0> state for use within the block scope. Qubits must be returned to |0> state before deallocation at scope exit.

```qsharp
operation MeasureAllZ(qubits : Qubit[]) : Result
```
Measures all qubits in the given array jointly in the Z-basis and returns a single Result, collapsing the joint quantum state to a computational basis state.

```qsharp
operation ApplyToEach<'T>(singleElementOperation : ('T => Unit), register : 'T[]) : Unit
```
Applies a single-qubit operation to every element in a qubit register array. A convenience operation for broadcasting gates across multiple qubits uniformly.

```qsharp
String
```
Text data type representing a sequence of characters.

```qsharp
Number
```
Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

```qsharp
Boolean
```
Logical type with two values: true and false.

```qsharp
Array
```
Ordered collection of elements accessible by index.

```qsharp
Object
```
Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).

```qsharp
null
```
Represents the intentional absence of any value.

```qsharp
undefined
```
Represents an uninitialized or missing value.
